methods.description: Boron isotope analyses were conducted using a Thermo Neptune Multi-Collector ICP-MS to determine isotopic composition, following established protocols for sample preparation and analysis.

steps.description: Boron isotope analyses were carried out on a Thermo Neptune Multi-Collector ICP-MS at the University of Southampton following Foster (2008).
steps.searchable_summary: perform boron isotope analysis using Thermo Neptune Multi-Collector ICP-MS

steps.description: External reproducibility is estimated from 11 B signal intensity using a relationship between intensity and long-term reproducibility.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate external reproducibility from 11 B signal intensity

methods.description: Sample cleaning and preparation follow established protocols to ensure accurate isotope and trace element analysis.

steps.description: Sample cleaning and preparation follow established protocols (see Anagnostou et al., 2016; Henehan et al., 2016, and references therein).
steps.searchable_summary: clean and prepare samples following established protocols

methods.description: Trace element analysis was conducted to measure concentrations of B, Mg, Al, and Sr using a Thermo Element ICP-MS.

steps.description: Trace element analysis was carried out using a Thermo Element ICP-MS at the University of Southampton.
steps.searchable_summary: perform trace element analysis using Thermo Element ICP-MS

steps.description: Long-term reproducibility (2 SD) was ±<5% for Al/Ca ratios measurements, and ±<3% for Mg/Ca and Sr/Ca.

methods.description: Stable isotope analyses for δ 13 C and δ 18 O were performed using mass spectrometers to determine isotopic ratios relative to standards.

steps.description: Foraminiferal δ 13 C and δ 18 O analyses from ODP Sites 702, 865, 1260, and 1263 were made on a Thermo Scientific Delta V Advantage mass spectrometer coupled to a GasBench II at Cardiff University.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ 13 C and δ 18 O using Thermo Scientific Delta V Advantage mass spectrometer
outputs: δ 13 C and δ 18 O data

steps.description: Reported relative to the Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite (VPDB) standard with an external analytical precision (1σ) of 0.06‰ for δ 13 C and 0.07‰ for δ 18 O.
steps.searchable_summary: validate isotope analysis against VPDB standard

steps.description: Further samples from IODP Site U1408 were measured for δ 13 C and δ 18 O on a Thermo Kiel IV coupled to a Thermo MAT 253 mass spectrometer at Yale University.
steps.searchable_summary: measure δ 13 C and δ 18 O using Thermo Kiel IV and Thermo MAT 253
outputs: δ 13 C and δ 18 O data

steps.description: Typical external precision (1σ) of <0.03‰ for δ 13 C and <0.04‰ for δ 18 O based on replicate analyses of in-house and international standards.
steps.searchable_summary: validate isotope analysis precision using standards
